description: Authoritative PASS/FAIL criteria for Rusty Dasher visual + input QA. Pair with scripts/qa_matrix.json (screens × formats). Agents MUST open this file before writing matrix_critique.md or video_critique.md. A cell may only use BAD: none when every applicable criterion here is satisfied. Rubber-stamping GOOD without opening each artifact is a process failure.

vocabulary.description: Two different meanings of PASS. Never conflate them.

CAPTURE_OK.means: Automation step succeeded: artifact written, state reached, steps counted, HTTP 200, matrix cell non-empty.
CAPTURE_OK.does_not_mean: Looks good, criteria passed, ship ready, A7 PASS.

REVIEW_PASS.means: Agent opened this-run artifacts, walked criteria, and recorded GOOD/BAD with evidence.
producers: human/agent image-tool review writing matrix_critique.md / video_critique.md

status_language: Say 'capture complete (CAPTURE_OK)' for runners — never 'A4 PASS' alone.
Say 'A4b/A6 reviewed; BAD: none' only after critique lines with opened-image evidence.
A7 is REVIEW only; CAPTURE_OK is a prerequisite, not the gate.

pre_prod_pass_requires: Every expected matrix cell has a CRITIQUE line
Every e2e recording has a VIDEO line
No open BAD where severity is blocker (or user accepted residual BADs in writing with IDs listed)
Artifact labels match actual GameState on the image/frame
Phase A handhelds used Android emulator + adb screenrecord + adb shell input (not Chrome emulate alone)

critique_format.matrix_line: CRITIQUE {format_id}_{shot_suffix}: GOOD: <evidence> | BAD: none | BAD: {criterion_id}: <what fails> [; {criterion_id}: ...]
critique_format.video_line: VIDEO {format_id}_{keyboard|mouse|touch}: GOOD: <evidence> | BAD: none | BAD: {criterion_id}: <what fails>
rules: GOOD and BAD both required on every line
BAD: none only after opening the artifact(s) and checking every applicable criterion in this file
When failing, cite criterion id(s) from this file (e.g. V-PLAY-NESTED-BORDER)
Listing a directory or trusting a prior agent PASS is not review
Filename alone is not proof of screen state — verify GameState from pixels/text

severity.blocker: Must be fixed or user-accepted in writing before push (Phase B) or claim ship
severity.major: Ship blocker unless user accepts residual in writing with criterion IDs
severity.minor: Should fix; may ship only if user accepts residual in writing

anti_patterns: Marking BAD: none because a previous critique said so without re-opening THIS run's artifacts
Accepting *_04_playing.png that shows Menu / ModeSelect / GameOver
Ignoring Phase C open_bads / events=0 because Phase A critiques were green
Treating Chrome page.emulate handhelds as ship-valid Phase A
Reviewing only boot/menu and skipping playing + landscape
Calling double blue frames or OOB stars 'intentional' without code proof + product sign-off
Treating suite CAPTURE_OK / exit 0 / N/N as A7 or as permission to write BAD: none
Status line '15/15 PASS open_bads none' without opened CRITIQUE/VIDEO evidence for this run
Calling A5 VERIFY (files present) a visual review

form_factor.description: How chrome and copy must match UiScale / ViewportClass (src/ui_scale.rs).

desktop.formats_note: All qa_matrix formats with touch:false (laptop_*, 1080p, qhd, 4k)

must: Full playfield (no Game Boy bottom deck / PSP side grips)
menu_copy_must: WASD / arrows / SPACE (or mouse point-to-move + right-click) language
Must not lead with stick-only / DASH-button-only copy

while_playing.portrait: Game Boy: playfield top; stick + DASH on bottom deck outside field
while_playing.landscape: PSP: stick left grip, DASH right grip, playfield center; controls outside field
must: Virtual stick visible and outside playfield rect
DASH button visible and outside playfield rect
menu_copy_must: Must not lead with WASD-only desktop copy

visual_criteria.description: Apply on every matrix PNG (A6) and relevant video stills (A4b). Any FAIL on a cell → that cell's BAD must list criterion ids.

global.check: No text clipped by panel edge, canvas edge, safe area, or another control
All primary labels readable at native capture size without guessing (title, CTAs, mode names, difficulty, score, lives, game-over stats, control hints)
global.notes: Phone landscape and laptop_hd are high-risk; zoom stills if needed

global.check: No unintended overlap of interactive controls or of status text by other UI chrome (START must not cover help; stick must not cover score)
No missing-glyph boxes, tofu rectangles, or broken substitute characters in UI strings. Prefer ASCII separators (- | /) if font lacks · • —
global.known_risk: ui.rs uses U+00B7 middle-dot (·) widely; default font often renders as empty boxes between words

global.check: No borders, field edges, dim slabs, or other sprites drawn through label glyphs
Not a panic screen, solid black/white empty frame, frozen boot mid-WASM with no CTA, or half-loaded layout

Artifact content matches claimed screen: boot shows boot CTA; menu shows RUSTY DASHER panel; mode_select shows SELECT MODE; playing shows Score/lives + field entities; game_over shows GAME OVER / NEW HIGH SCORE. Wrong state with right filename = FAIL
Menu/mode/game-over panels fully inside the game canvas; not cut by browser chrome in a way that removes required controls (START, swap band, again hints)

While menu / mode_select / game_over is up: no empty blue playfield rectangle ghosts peeking above/below/beside the panel; playfield border must not fight the panel (full dim or hide FieldPiece border/glow under overlays)
known_fail_examples: phone_portrait_02_menu.png — blue rects above and below panel
phone_landscape_02_menu.png — framed empty boxes left and right of panel

global.check: On-screen control copy matches form_factor (desktop vs handheld) for this matrix unit
While playing: handheld shows stick+DASH outside field; desktop shows neither stick nor DASH button

playing.check: Exactly one coherent playfield border rect. No nested double/triple blue frames (outer + inner misaligned polygons)
No semi-opaque dark vertical (or horizontal) slabs covering the left/right (or top/bottom) interior of the playfield, dimming stars/hazards under chrome
playing.notes: PSP grips and Game Boy deck must sit OUTSIDE the play rect, not as translucent overlays on field content

playing.check: Player, stars, hazards, powerups render inside the playfield border (or intentionally clipped at the border edge). Not free-floating in grip chrome, under stick, or outside the outer blue rect

No unexplained geometry: double frames, stray blue half-disks cutting the border, random rectangles, mesh tears, or debug-looking shapes. FieldPiece::Glow must not read as a glitch half-circle outside the border
playing.notes: If glow is intentional VFX it must stay aesthetically inside design intent and not break border readability; otherwise clip/hide

playing.check: Score, hearts, status/level line, Dash READY/cooldown remain legible and not covered by stick/DASH or cut by field border mid-glyph
Hazards/player should not persistently sit on top of the status line as if HUD and world share broken z/layout (occasional transit OK; stuck-on-label is BAD)
Handheld: stick base and DASH circle fully outside the blue playfield rect with clear separation (not overlapping field interior)
Desktop/laptop formats: no stick, no DASH button while playing

mode_select.check: All four modes visible or navigable (Classic, Zen, Survival, Timed) and difficulty control readable (EASY…INSANE or compact chevrons)
START control fully visible and not covering help/footer text; help text not clipped under START

game_over.check: GAME OVER or NEW HIGH SCORE title fully unclipped; stats and again/menu hints readable and form-factor-correct

boot.check: Settled boot: title + dismiss CTA present (not mid-download blank). CTA text may mention ENTER/click/tap

input_criteria.description: Exhaustive surface required on every e2e path (A4 / Phase C). Failures are ship blockers.

must_exercise: Handheld: stick/DASH swap band on menu when present
Mode select: change mode and difficulty, then START
Game over / Esc back when in scope

per_path.check: Boot CTA dismisses via keyboard, mouse/click, and touch as applicable; canvas receives input after
Menu advances to mode select via confirm for the path's input modality
All modes and difficulties can be selected (not stuck on one option)
START enters Playing; play is not frozen black
Movement works: keys / mouse drag / virtual stick; events non-zero when path claims play input
Dash works: Space / right-click / DASH button; cooldown or trail evidence acceptable
≥ min_play_ms continuous play visible on recording (not truncated before 20s unless force_go path documents otherwise for matrix GO shot only)
Instrumented touch/pointer paths must not report events=0 for stick and DASH after calibrated input (Phase C inventory and emulator adb paths)
known_fail_examples: screenshots/web/phone/touch_inventory.md 2026-07-11: stick/dash FAIL events=0 on all 2×2 cells

per_path.check: Hit diameter ≥ 48 CSS px; stick↔dash gap ≥ 12 CSS px on handheld play layouts

video_criteria.description: A4b — continuous playthrough review. Stills help but are not a substitute for sampling the recording when available.

checks.check: Recording shows boot→menu→mode→play→(game over when in scope) without panic/blank/stuck boot
Visible response to move and dash; handheld stick thumb moves; desktop no stick chrome
No obvious flicker, wrong-screen flash, or control dead-zones mid-run
Status/HUD during play not unusable (clipping mid-run is BAD)
If matrix PNGs fail a visual criterion, matching video stills must not be marked BAD: none — same visual rules apply

artifact_criteria.description: Integrity of capture outputs — wrong labels and empty proofs are FAILs even if 'something' was saved.

checks.check: All expected_cells from qa_matrix.json exist, non-empty, under screenshots/viewports/{format}_{shot_suffix}.png
Each required matrix unit × input path has a primary recording (webm/mp4) for the ship path under review
File names and inventory rows that claim playing|menu|mode|game_over must match the visible state. *_04_playing that shows menu = FAIL
Phase C when device present: portrait|landscape × browsing|fullscreen all run; inventory written; open_bads must be 0 or user-accepted by id
Every matrix cell and every recording has a critique line for THIS run (mtime/run id evidence preferred)

pass_if: All applicable visual_criteria + input_criteria + video_criteria for Phase A have no open blocker/major BADs (unless user-accepted)
fail_if_any: Any CRITIQUE/VIDEO line still has unaccepted BAD
Rubber-stamp evidence (critiques claim GOOD while known_fail_examples still present on this-run artifacts)

C_physical_phone.requires: B deploy success + physical adb device
C_physical_phone.fail_sends_to: Phase A fix loop (not phone-only push)

agent_instructions: In status files, label capture vs review explicitly (CAPTURE_OK vs A4b/A6 reviewed).
CAPTURE_OK (scripts) ≠ REVIEW. Never mark BAD: none solely because capture printed PASS/CAPTURE_OK.
Before any A4b/A6 critique line: open scripts/qa_success_criteria.json and scripts/qa_matrix.json.
Open each artifact with the image tool (or sample video stills). Directory listing is not review.
For each screen, walk review_checklist_by_screen and mark FAIL with criterion id if any check fails.
If unsure whether a shape is intentional VFX, FAIL major and ask — do not invent BAD: none.
When this file and a skill text disagree on PASS/FAIL, this file wins for criteria; skills win for process/order.
Update this file when new regression classes are found (add criterion id + known_fail_examples) — do not only mention them in chat.
